objective: Measure graphify usage and compare graphify-guided retrieval against baseline repo-search retrieval on real fail-fast scenarios, while defining an auditable event path for graphify usage across governed workflows.
scope_boundary: Add a governance-hosted measurement harness for graphify-vs-baseline retrieval on real fail-fast scenarios.
Add a documented usage-event schema and logger for graphify session usage.
Persist comparable metrics and summary artifacts in governance.
out_of_scope: Automatic interception of every Codex or Claude graphify invocation.
Model-based answer scoring or external evaluator infrastructure.
Solving graphify retrieval quality itself beyond measuring it.
research_summary: The open fail-fast issues already provide concrete retrieval tasks: reusable workflow bug discovery, raw-feed redaction, weekly sweep persistence visibility, and Codex qualification validation. That corpus is enough to compare graphify-guided retrieval to normal repo-search retrieval in a repeatable way. The bounded first slice should produce governance-hosted scenarios, a measurement script, an auditable usage-event path, and tracked result snapshots; issue #66 remains the quality-improvement follow-up if the measured graphify strategy underperforms baseline search.

sprints.name: Sprint 1
sprints.goal: Create a repeatable graphify-vs-baseline retrieval harness.
tasks: Define a scenario corpus using the real fail-fast issues.
Implement graphify-guided retrieval and baseline repo-search retrieval strategies.
Persist JSON and Markdown results with quality and token-footprint estimates.
acceptance_criteria: Governance can run at least one repeatable graphify-vs-search comparison locally.
Results persist in tracked governance metrics/artifacts.
The harness uses real fail-fast scenarios, not synthetic prompts.

sprints.name: Sprint 2
sprints.goal: Add an auditable path for graphify usage events.
tasks: Add a simple logger that appends JSONL usage events.
Document when governed workflows should record graphify usage.
acceptance_criteria: Governed repos have a documented path to log graphify usage events.
Usage events can be written as append-only tracked artifacts.
Docs reflect that graphify-vs-baseline measurement is now auditable.

specialist_reviews.focus: Keep the first slice deterministic and repo-local: compare retrieval quality and estimated token footprint without requiring a model-in-the-loop grader.
specialist_reviews.summary: A deterministic harness over real fail-fast scenarios is the right first measurement step. Quality should be defined as expected-file retrieval success and retrieval footprint, with issue #66 left to improve graphify quality after baseline data exists.

alternate_model_review.summary: This slice builds the measurement harness and does not require alternate-model review.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Build a deterministic graphify-vs-baseline harness using the fail-fast issues, add append-only graphify usage logging, persist results, and wire docs/backlog to the new measurement contract.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the scenario corpus, measurement script, usage logger, and tracked result artifacts; run the harness locally; then package the governance PR.

material_deviation_rules: If deterministic retrieval scoring proves too weak to answer the quality question, keep this slice focused on instrumentation and open a follow-up rather than overfitting the harness.
If the results show graphify quality gaps, keep issue #66 open as the quality-improvement follow-up and reference the measured evidence.
